name: Growth & Paid
description: Your Growth & Paid hire. I plan paid campaigns (Google / Meta / LinkedIn), monitor competitor ads, draft ad copy from real customer language, critique landing pages, design A/B tests, spec event-tracking + UTM plans, and run the weekly funnel review.
author: Houston Founder Marketing

[{"id":"weekly-funnel-readout","name":"Weekly funnel readout","description":"Monday funnel check — flags the biggest drop and recommends experiments if something moved materially.","prompt":"Run the weekly funnel readout using the analyze-funnel skill.

1. Read ../head-of-marketing/product-marketing-context.md. If it's missing or empty, state that positioning is missing and stop — do NOT surface activity unless user action is required.
2. Read config/ to determine the connected analytics provider (PostHog / GA4 / Mixpanel / Amplitude / Segment) and the primary conversion event.
3. If the required analytics integration isn't connected via Composio, state which category is missing and stop — do NOT surface activity unless user action is required.
4. Pull last-7-days and trailing-4-week conversion at each funnel step, compare to the prior 4 weeks.
5. Baseline check: if funnel-reviews/baseline.json does not exist, write current conversion rates to funnel-reviews/baseline.json, note this is the first run, and end with ROUTINE_OK.
6. Otherwise, compare to baseline + prior weeks. Only surface if there is a material change (>15% relative drop in any step, a new leak, or a step that has degraded 3+ weeks in a row). Write the readout to funnel-reviews/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log in outputs.json with 2-3 experiments ranked by lift × confidence ÷ effort.
7. If nothing material changed, still refresh baseline.json with this week's numbers and finish silently.","schedule":"0 9 * * 1","enabled":true,"suppress_when_silent":true,"created_at":"2026-04-23T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:00:00Z"},{"id":"weekly-competitor-ad-scan","name":"Weekly competitor ad scan","description":"Friday scan of competitor ad libraries — surfaces new angles worth responding to.","prompt":"Scan competitor ads using the monitor-competitor-ads skill.

1. Read ../head-of-marketing/product-marketing-context.md. If missing or empty, state that positioning is missing and stop — do NOT surface activity unless user action is required.
2. Read config/ for the competitor list. If no competitors are configured, stop and state that no competitors are configured — do NOT surface activity unless user action is required.
3. If the required web-scrape or ad-library integration isn't connected via Composio, state which category is missing and stop — do NOT surface activity unless user action is required.
4. Pull live creative from Meta Ad Library, LinkedIn Ad Library, and Google Ads Transparency for each configured competitor.
5. Baseline check: if competitor-ads/baseline.json does not exist, record each competitor's current active ad set (angles + hooks + ad ids) to competitor-ads/baseline.json and end with ROUTINE_OK.
6. Otherwise, diff against baseline. Only surface if a competitor launched 2+ new angles this week, shifted funnel stage, or dropped a recurring angle. Write the diff to competitor-ads/weekly-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md with a 'steal this' list of 2-3 angles we should test and log in outputs.json.
7. Always refresh baseline.json with this week's snapshot.","schedule":"0 16 * * 5","enabled":true,"suppress_when_silent":true,"created_at":"2026-04-23T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:00:00Z"}]

useCases.title: Plan a Google Ads search campaign end-to-end
useCases.blurb: Audience, keywords, budget, KPIs — agency-ready brief.
useCases.prompt: Plan a Google Ads search campaign for {keyword cluster}.
useCases.fullPrompt: Plan a Google Ads search campaign for {keyword cluster}. Use the plan-paid-campaign skill. Before writing, read the positioning doc and my config (channels, analytics, primary conversion). I want a full brief I could hand to an agency: audience + intent, keyword/placement strategy with negatives, ad-group structure, budget at three tiers (lean / base / aggressive), landing-page requirements, tracking + UTM plan, and KPI targets tied to my primary conversion. Save it to campaigns/google-ads-{slug}.md and log it in outputs.json. Be opinionated — tell me which ad group to launch first.
useCases.description: Full brief: audience, keyword/placement strategy, ad-group structure, suggested budget, landing-page requirements, KPI targets. Ready to hand to an agency or run yourself.
useCases.outcome: Campaign brief at campaigns/{channel}-{slug}.md — spec the experiment before you spend a dollar.

useCases.title: Teardown a competitor's ads
useCases.blurb: Their live angles, hooks, and what to steal.
useCases.prompt: Teardown {competitor}'s current ads from Meta Ad Library and LinkedIn Ad Library.
useCases.fullPrompt: Pull {competitor}'s live ad creative from Meta Ad Library, LinkedIn Ad Library, and Google Ads Transparency via web-scrape. Use the monitor-competitor-ads skill. For each ad identify the angle, hook, offer, and which stage of the funnel it targets. Group by repeated angles (those are their bets). Write the teardown to competitor-ads/{competitor}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md. Close with a 'steal this' list of 3 angles I should test against them — tied back to our positioning. Log it in outputs.json.
useCases.description: Pull their live creative from Meta Ad Library / LinkedIn Ad Library / Google Ads Transparency. Extract the angles, hooks, and offers they're testing.
useCases.outcome: Ad teardown at competitor-ads/{competitor}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md — the best research for your own ad copy.

useCases.title: Draft ad copy grounded in real customer language
useCases.blurb: 10 variants — each cites the review quote behind it.
useCases.prompt: Draft 10 ad variants grounded in real customer language from G2 reviews.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft 10 ad variants for {campaign or product} grounded in actual customer language. Use the generate-ad-copy skill. Pull phrases from G2, Capterra, Trustpilot, and relevant Reddit threads via web-scrape. For each of the 10 variants write headline + description and cite the source quote that inspired it — verbatim, with link. No invented angles. No marketing adjectives that don't appear somewhere in the reviews. Save to ad-copy/{campaign-slug}.md and log it in outputs.json. Flag the 3 I should launch first and why.
useCases.description: Pull phrases from G2 / Capterra / Trustpilot (via web scrape) and write headlines + descriptions that sound like your customers talking — not like a marketer pitching.
useCases.outcome: Variants at ad-copy/{campaign-slug}.md with the source quote alongside each headline.

useCases.title: Rigorously critique a landing page
useCases.blurb: 6 dimensions scored, fixes ranked by lift × effort.
useCases.prompt: Critique the landing page at {url}. Score each dimension and give me a fix list.
useCases.fullPrompt: I want a rigorous CRO teardown of {url}. Use the critique-landing-page skill and fetch the page via Firecrawl. Score 6 dimensions 0–3: headline clarity, value prop, social proof, CTA, objection handling, visual hierarchy. Call out above-the-fold vs below-the-fold separately. Then give me a prioritized fix list — each fix with the expected lift direction, effort (S/M/L), and the exact copy or element change. Save to cro-critiques/{url-slug}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log in outputs.json. Don't hedge — I'll ship the top 3 fixes this week.
useCases.description: Fetch via Firecrawl. Score 6 dimensions 0–3: headline clarity, value prop, social proof, CTA, objection handling, visual hierarchy. Then a prioritized fix list — not a generic lecture.
useCases.outcome: Teardown at cro-critiques/{url-slug}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md. Copy the fixes into your tracker.

useCases.title: Design a proper A/B test, not a coin flip
useCases.blurb: Hypothesis, MDE, power, go/no-go — a real spec.
useCases.prompt: Design an A/B test for the pricing page headline.
useCases.fullPrompt: Design a proper A/B test for {change — e.g. the pricing page headline}. Use the design-ab-test skill. I want a full spec I could ship to engineering: hypothesis in PICOT form, the control and variant rendered as copy (or mockup pseudo-code), primary and secondary metrics tied to my analytics stack, sample-size estimate with the MDE and power I'm assuming, expected duration at current traffic, and go/no-go criteria. Call out risks and confounds. Save to ab-tests/{slug}.md and log in outputs.json. If traffic is too low for a real test, say so and suggest a qualitative alternative.
useCases.description: Full spec: hypothesis (PICOT), control vs variant, primary + secondary metrics, sample-size estimate with MDE + power, duration, go/no-go criteria — so you don't ship the loser.
useCases.outcome: Test spec at ab-tests/{slug}.md. Paste into your experimentation tool.

useCases.title: Spec event tracking + a UTM matrix
useCases.prompt: Spec the event tracking plan for sign-up → activation with GA4 and give me a UTM naming matrix.
useCases.fullPrompt: Spec the full event-tracking plan for sign-up → activation in GA4 (or my configured analytics tool — check config/). Use the setup-tracking skill. Define each event: name, trigger condition, required properties, owner on eng, and the funnel step it maps to. Then give me a UTM naming matrix so paid / social / email / referral are all comparable downstream — with concrete examples per channel. Save to tracking-plans/{slug}.md and log it in outputs.json. I'm going to hand this directly to engineering, so be exact.
useCases.description: Event names, triggers, properties, and owner per step. A UTM matrix so paid / social / email are comparable in GA4.
useCases.outcome: Plan at tracking-plans/{slug}.md you can hand to engineering.

useCases.title: Weekly funnel readout — find the leak
useCases.blurb: Biggest drop + 2–3 experiments ranked by lift/effort.
useCases.prompt: Give me the weekly funnel readout from PostHog — where are we leaking?
useCases.fullPrompt: Run the weekly funnel readout. Use the analyze-funnel skill. Pull last-7-days and trailing-4-week conversion at each step from my connected analytics (PostHog, GA4, or Mixpanel — whatever's in config/). Compare to the prior 4 weeks. Flag the biggest leak and whether it's new. Recommend 2–3 experiments ranked by expected lift × confidence ÷ effort — each with a one-line hypothesis. Save to funnel-reviews/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log it in outputs.json. No dashboard dumps — I want a decision, not a view.
useCases.description: Compute conversion at each step from connected analytics (PostHog / GA4 / Mixpanel). Flag the biggest drop, recommend 2–3 experiments ranked by expected lift vs effort.
useCases.outcome: Review at funnel-reviews/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md — clear next actions, not a dashboard dump.
